All al.com links opened in FB detect adguard and I get the pop up telling me to disable it. This is new and only started about a month ago. Prior to this, I had no problem opening the links in FB with adguard enabled. > > You recently fixed a similar issue with al.com links not opening in the browser when adguard was enabled. If I direct fb to open the link externally, it will open without a problem. ### Screenshots

issue.body:
### Issue URL (Incorrect Blocking) [https://adguard.com/ko/welcome.html](https://adguardteam.github.io/AnonymousRedirect/redirect.html?url=https%3A%2F%2Fadguard.com%2Fko%2Fwelcome.html) ### Comment > Since yesterday, enabling AdGuard has caused all internet connectivity to stop working. I tried disabling each of the four protections individually — ad blocking, tracking protection, DNS protection, and the firewall — and also turned them all off at the same time, but the internet remains unavailable whenever AdGuard is turned on. This affects not only Chrome but also messaging apps, which are unable to send messages due to the loss of internet connection. The issue persists after multiple updates, which is why I am submitting this error report. ### Screenshots
Screenshot 1 ![Screenshot 1](https://cdn.adguardcdn.com/sitereports/l7rr20pakf4kgk8cs8gsco0c8wokcg0pp3w4v3bjs0.jpg?nc=1)

issue.title: Google Play: in.amazon.mShop.android.shopping

labels.name: T: Incorrect Blocking
labels.description: Website or app not working properly due to filtering

issue.body:
### Issue URL (Incorrect Blocking)
[https://play.google.com/store/apps/details?id=in.amazon.mShop.android.shopping](https://adguardteam.github.io/AnonymousRedirect/redirect.html?url=https%3A%2F%2Fplay.google.com%2Fstore%2Fapps%2Fdetails%3Fid%3Din.amazon.mShop.android.shopping)

### Comment
> 🔹If i set dns service to dns.adguard-dns.com or family.adguard-dns.com or unfiltered server in device settings i can browse all apps except amazon (india) app normally. Even amazon website (.in/.com) loads properly in browser. Amazon app shows an unending loading screen as shown in attached screenshots.
> 🔹I also noticed some difficulty occasionally while downloading ad-ons in Firefox browser.
> 🔹 I tried device restart etc. but it does not fix this issue.
> 🔹 Even dns check shows server is connected (singapore).
> 🔹 Using alternate services like quad9 or controlD works fine. Even with private dns turned off, this issue does not happen.
> 🔹 This issue has been happening from past 4 days.



### Screenshots
<details>
  <summary>Screenshot 1</summary>

  ![Screenshot 1](https://cdn.adguardcdn.com/sitereports/8ucc2wzqchcskgkccskk0kck4kswwowec0ebkgqgio.jpg?nc=1)
</details>

<details>
  <summary>Screenshot 1 with AdGuard disabled</summary>

  ![Screenshot 1](https://cdn.adguardcdn.com/sitereports/axz3qghpa1skcgk4k08skws0owkwcwcen52vy9q5xk.jpg?nc=1)
</details>

### System configuration

| Information | Value |
|-------------|-------|
AdGuard product: | AdGuard DNS
DNS setup: | Router
DNS server: | Default

System version: | `android 12`
Browser: | Firefox

[https://play.google.com/store/apps/details?id=in.amazon.mShop.android.shopping](https://adguardteam.github.io/AnonymousRedirect/redirect.html?url=https%3A%2F%2Fplay.google.com%2Fstore%2Fapps%2Fdetails%3Fid%3Din.amazon.mShop.android.shopping)

### Comment
> 🔹If i set dns service to dns.adguard-dns.com or family.adguard-dns.com or unfiltered server in device settings i can browse all apps except amazon (india) app normally. Even amazon website (.in/.com) loads properly in browser. Amazon app shows an unending loading screen as shown in attached screenshots.
> 🔹I also noticed some difficulty occasionally while downloading ad-ons in Firefox browser.
> 🔹 I tried device restart etc. but it does not fix this issue.
> 🔹 Even dns check shows server is connected (singapore).
> 🔹 Using alternate services like quad9 or controlD works fine. Even with private dns turned off, this issue does not happen.
> 🔹 This issue has been happening from past 4 days.



### Screenshots
<details>
  <summary>Screenshot 1</summary>

  ![Screenshot 1](https://cdn.adguardcdn.com/sitereports/8ucc2wzqchcskgkccskk0kck4kswwowec0ebkgqgio.jpg?nc=1)
</details>

  ![Screenshot 1](https://cdn.adguardcdn.com/sitereports/axz3qghpa1skcgk4k08skws0owkwcwcen52vy9q5xk.jpg?nc=1)
</details>

review.body:
> utm_compaign is blocked by generic rule.

I didn't find `utm_compaign` in generic filters. ([Code search result `repo:AdguardTeam/AdguardFilters utm_compaign`](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3AAdguardTeam%2FAdguardFilters%20utm_compaign&type=code)). Therefore, I separate rules for each parameter regarding your feedback.

> Alex: MV3 extension does not support regexp in this modifier. `$removeparam=aaa|bbb` also not supported. 
>
> Devs recommends to use separate rules for each parameter. This also makes it easier to troubleshoot rules when issues arise due to a rule.

> utm_compaign is blocked by generic rule.

I didn't find `utm_compaign` in generic filters. ([Code search result `repo:AdguardTeam/AdguardFilters utm_compaign`](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3AAdguardTeam%2FAdguardFilters%20utm_compaign&type=code)). Therefore, I separate rules for each parameter regarding your feedback.

> Alex: MV3 extension does not support regexp in this modifier. `$removeparam=aaa|bbb` also not supported. 
>
> Devs recommends to use separate rules for each parameter. This also makes it easier to troubleshoot rules when issues arise due to a rule.

> utm_compaign is blocked by generic rule.

I didn't find `utm_compaign` in generic filters. ([Code search result `repo:AdguardTeam/AdguardFilters utm_compaign`](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3AAdguardTeam%2FAdguardFilters%20utm_compaign&type=code)). Therefore, I separate rules for each parameter regarding your feedback.

> Alex: MV3 extension does not support regexp in this modifier. `$removeparam=aaa|bbb` also not supported. 
>
> Devs recommends to use separate rules for each parameter. This also makes it easier to troubleshoot rules when issues arise due to a rule.

issue.title: Google Play: in.amazon.mShop.android.shopping

labels.name: T: Incorrect Blocking
labels.description: Website or app not working properly due to filtering

labels.name: A: Cannot reproduce
labels.description: Filters devs cannot reproduce this issue

labels.name: A: In progress
labels.description: Work on the issue is in progress

labels.name: A: Waiting for data
labels.description: Waiting for more information from the user

issue.body:
### Issue URL (Incorrect Blocking)
[https://play.google.com/store/apps/details?id=in.amazon.mShop.android.shopping](https://adguardteam.github.io/AnonymousRedirect/redirect.html?url=https%3A%2F%2Fplay.google.com%2Fstore%2Fapps%2Fdetails%3Fid%3Din.amazon.mShop.android.shopping)

### Comment
> 🔹If i set dns service to dns.adguard-dns.com or family.adguard-dns.com or unfiltered server in device settings i can browse all apps except amazon (india) app normally. Even amazon website (.in/.com) loads properly in browser. Amazon app shows an unending loading screen as shown in attached screenshots.
> 🔹I also noticed some difficulty occasionally while downloading ad-ons in Firefox browser.
> 🔹 I tried device restart etc. but it does not fix this issue.
> 🔹 Even dns check shows server is connected (singapore).
> 🔹 Using alternate services like quad9 or controlD works fine. Even with private dns turned off, this issue does not happen.
> 🔹 This issue has been happening from past 4 days.



### Screenshots
<details>
  <summary>Screenshot 1</summary>

  ![Screenshot 1](https://cdn.adguardcdn.com/sitereports/8ucc2wzqchcskgkccskk0kck4kswwowec0ebkgqgio.jpg?nc=1)
</details>

<details>
  <summary>Screenshot 1 with AdGuard disabled</summary>

  ![Screenshot 1](https://cdn.adguardcdn.com/sitereports/axz3qghpa1skcgk4k08skws0owkwcwcen52vy9q5xk.jpg?nc=1)
</details>

### System configuration

| Information | Value |
|-------------|-------|
AdGuard product: | AdGuard DNS
DNS setup: | Router
DNS server: | Default

System version: | `android 12`
Browser: | Firefox

@AdguardTeam/filters-maintainers please also check this out.

### Issue URL (Incorrect Blocking)
[https://play.google.com/store/apps/details?id=in.amazon.mShop.android.shopping](https://adguardteam.github.io/AnonymousRedirect/redirect.html?url=https%3A%2F%2Fplay.google.com%2Fstore%2Fapps%2Fdetails%3Fid%3Din.amazon.mShop.android.shopping)

### Comment
> 🔹If i set dns service to dns.adguard-dns.com or family.adguard-dns.com or unfiltered server in device settings i can browse all apps except amazon (india) app normally. Even amazon website (.in/.com) loads properly in browser. Amazon app shows an unending loading screen as shown in attached screenshots.
> 🔹I also noticed some difficulty occasionally while downloading ad-ons in Firefox browser.
> 🔹 I tried device restart etc. but it does not fix this issue.
> 🔹 Even dns check shows server is connected (singapore).
> 🔹 Using alternate services like quad9 or controlD works fine. Even with private dns turned off, this issue does not happen.
> 🔹 This issue has been happening from past 4 days.

issue.body:
### Issue URL (Incorrect Blocking)
[https://play.google.com/store/apps/details?id=in.amazon.mShop.android.shopping](https://adguardteam.github.io/AnonymousRedirect/redirect.html?url=https%3A%2F%2Fplay.google.com%2Fstore%2Fapps%2Fdetails%3Fid%3Din.amazon.mShop.android.shopping)

### Comment
> 🔹If i set dns service to dns.adguard-dns.com or family.adguard-dns.com or unfiltered server in device settings i can browse all apps except amazon (india) app normally. Even amazon website (.in/.com) loads properly in browser. Amazon app shows an unending loading screen as shown in attached screenshots.
> 🔹I also noticed some difficulty occasionally while downloading ad-ons in Firefox browser.
> 🔹 I tried device restart etc. but it does not fix this issue.
> 🔹 Even dns check shows server is connected (singapore).
> 🔹 Using alternate services like quad9 or controlD works fine. Even with private dns turned off, this issue does not happen.
> 🔹 This issue has been happening from past 4 days.



### Screenshots
<details>
  <summary>Screenshot 1</summary>

  ![Screenshot 1](https://cdn.adguardcdn.com/sitereports/8ucc2wzqchcskgkccskk0kck4kswwowec0ebkgqgio.jpg?nc=1)
</details>

<details>
  <summary>Screenshot 1 with AdGuard disabled</summary>

  ![Screenshot 1](https://cdn.adguardcdn.com/sitereports/axz3qghpa1skcgk4k08skws0owkwcwcen52vy9q5xk.jpg?nc=1)
</details>

### System configuration

| Information | Value |
|-------------|-------|
AdGuard product: | AdGuard DNS
DNS setup: | Router
DNS server: | Default

System version: | `android 12`
Browser: | Firefox

comment.body: Hello i am original author of this ticket above, i am facing this issue with all filters as i mentioned above like 95% of the time. I even toggle aeroplane mode to try to refresh the servers (even device restarts does not fix it). But it is most of the time not working. It is not even letting me browse amazon (.in/.com) via browser. I the first comment, i had mentioned that i was able to browse amazon via browser but i had failed to mention that i have separately been using [https://dns.adguard-dns.com/dns-query] manually in firefox android as dns provider. It is strange that if i change DNSto default values in Firefox android :  Cloudflare/Nextdns or even off, amazon website can be browsed without an issue [including even if https://dns.adguard-dns.com/dns-query] is used. So i am a bit confused why the above mentioned dns from adguard lets normal usage of amazon website while, only setting the dns value to any of the 3 filters [dns.adguard-dns.com/family.adguard-dns.com/unfiltered.adguard-dns.com] in device settings causes this issue [These filters plus firefox dns to https://dns.adguard-dns.com/dns-query does not cause issues in browser while browsing amazon. Thanks a lot.

issue.body:
### Issue URL (Anti Adblock Script) [https://img.verb.tw/home/upload/](https://adguardteam.github.io/AnonymousRedirect/redirect.html?url=https%3A%2F%2Fimg.verb.tw%2Fhome%2Fupload%2F) ### Comment > Issue: The site uses a bait-element technique to detect ad blockers and blocks the upload feature when an ad blocker is active. > > How it works: > > The page runs a JavaScript snippet that: > > 1. Checks if adsbygoogle.js was blocked (by looking for script[src*="adsbygoogle"] in the DOM). > 2. Creates a hidden div off-screen with class names commonly targeted by ad blocker filter lists: adsbygoogle ad-slot ad-banner textads banner-ads, plus fake data-ad-client and data-ad-slot attributes. > 3. After 500ms, checks whether the bait element was removed from the DOM, or has offsetHeight/offsetWidth === 0, or has display:none / visibility:hidden applied by the ad blocker. > 4. If any condition is true, the upload UI is hidden and replaced with a message: "Ad blocker detected - ads are our only income, please disable your ad blocker and refresh." > > The bait element is never a real ad - it is purely a decoy used to detect and penalize users with ad blockers. ### Screenshots
Screenshot 1 ![Screenshot 1](https://cdn.adguardcdn.com/sitereports/jm7g57i37fcc80c8o08ocwck880o8ggqxp83s4g0g0.png?nc=1)

Checks if adsbygoogle.js was blocked (by looking for script[src*="adsbygoogle"] in the DOM). > 2. Creates a hidden div off-screen with class names commonly targeted by ad blocker filter lists: adsbygoogle ad-slot ad-banner textads banner-ads, plus fake data-ad-client and data-ad-slot attributes. > 3. After 500ms, checks whether the bait element was removed from the DOM, or has offsetHeight/offsetWidth === 0, or has display:none / visibility:hidden applied by the ad blocker. > 4. If any condition is true, the upload UI is hidden and replaced with a message: "Ad blocker detected - ads are our only income, please disable your ad blocker and refresh." > > The bait element is never a real ad - it is purely a decoy used to detect and penalize users with ad blockers. ### Screenshots
Screenshot 1 ![Screenshot 1](https://cdn.adguardcdn.com/sitereports/jm7g57i37fcc80c8o08ocwck880o8ggqxp83s4g0g0.png?nc=1)

comment.body:
This issue has been automatically marked as stale because it has been open for 3 days with no activity.
Unfortunately, our resources are limited and we can not always take the time to respond to requests about websites that are not popular enough. The issue will be closed if no further activity occurs.
Please note, that AdGuard Filters is an open-source project that is used by different content blockers and we welcome contributions. If you have some technical knowledge about how websites work, you are welcome to create a pull request following [these instructions](https://github.com/AdguardTeam/AdguardFilters/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md).

issue.body:
### Issue URL (Incorrect Blocking)
[https://play.google.com/store/apps/details?id=in.amazon.mShop.android.shopping](https://adguardteam.github.io/AnonymousRedirect/redirect.html?url=https%3A%2F%2Fplay.google.com%2Fstore%2Fapps%2Fdetails%3Fid%3Din.amazon.mShop.android.shopping)

### Comment
> 🔹If i set dns service to dns.adguard-dns.com or family.adguard-dns.com or unfiltered server in device settings i can browse all apps except amazon (india) app normally. Even amazon website (.in/.com) loads properly in browser. Amazon app shows an unending loading screen as shown in attached screenshots.
> 🔹I also noticed some difficulty occasionally while downloading ad-ons in Firefox browser.
> 🔹 I tried device restart etc. but it does not fix this issue.
> 🔹 Even dns check shows server is connected (singapore).
> 🔹 Using alternate services like quad9 or controlD works fine. Even with private dns turned off, this issue does not happen.
> 🔹 This issue has been happening from past 4 days.



### Screenshots
<details>
  <summary>Screenshot 1</summary>

  ![Screenshot 1](https://cdn.adguardcdn.com/sitereports/8ucc2wzqchcskgkccskk0kck4kswwowec0ebkgqgio.jpg?nc=1)
</details>

<details>
  <summary>Screenshot 1 with AdGuard disabled</summary>

  ![Screenshot 1](https://cdn.adguardcdn.com/sitereports/axz3qghpa1skcgk4k08skws0owkwcwcen52vy9q5xk.jpg?nc=1)
</details>

### System configuration

| Information | Value |
|-------------|-------|
AdGuard product: | AdGuard DNS
DNS setup: | Router
DNS server: | Default

System version: | `android 12`
Browser: | Firefox

comment.body:
🪶In short:
🔹Setting device private dns to unfiltered.adguard-dns.com/family.adguard-dns.com/dns.adguard-dns.com = amazon app / website (https://www.amazon.in/) not working [95% of time].
🔹Setting device private dns as above + using https://dns.adguard-dns.com/dns-query as resolver in firefox android browser (or other alternatives cloudflare/nextdns) = amazon website loads perfectly on firefox while amazon app does not work. [Super weird🙄]
🔹Device private dns off or cloudflare, controlD etc= working (app & website) (dns off or others in browser)
🔹Issue since past 4 days.

comment.body:
Got it. I thought you were talking about the app. I confirmed on my browser. 

Also, I found GCP and AWS tracker endpoints:

- `gcp.api.snapchat.com/web-blizzard/web/send`:
  - Analytics/telemetry (Snapchat's "Blizzard" pipeline)
  - Called 5× during login; protobuf POST; different subdomain from the already-blocked `web.snapchat.com/web-blizzard/web/send`
- `aws.api.snapchat.com/snapchat.cdp.cof.CircumstancesService/targetingQuery`:
  - CDP audience targeting; gRPC-web; called 2×
- `aws.api.snapchat.com/snapchat.ab.exposure_service.ExposureService/BatchUpdateAbExposure`:
  - A/B test exposure logging
  - gRPC-web; payload includes experiment key `GOOGLE_OAUTH_CONSUMER_LOGIN_AB__188607`

Thanks for your reporting 😇 Please do not close this issue until it is applied to master.

> [!NOTE]
> This issue is a continuation of #215701

comment.body:
This issue has been automatically marked as stale because it has been open for 3 days with no activity.
Unfortunately, our resources are limited and we can not always take the time to respond to requests about websites that are not popular enough. The issue will be closed if no further activity occurs.
Please note, that AdGuard Filters is an open-source project that is used by different content blockers and we welcome contributions. If you have some technical knowledge about how websites work, you are welcome to create a pull request following [these instructions](https://github.com/AdguardTeam/AdguardFilters/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md).

issue.body:
### Prerequisites

- [x] This site DOES NOT contain sexually explicit material, otherwise use [NSFW-specific form](./new?template=bug_report_NSFW.yml);
- [x] Filters were updated before reproducing an issue;
- [x] AdGuard product version is up-to-date;
- [x] Browser version is up-to-date;
- [x] If the site or app is broken, disabling AdGuard protection resolves an issue.

### What product do you use?

AdGuard for Android

Missed analytics or tracker

Brave

Mobile

AdGuard Base filter

### Privacy

comment.body:
> Got it. I thought you were talking about the app. I confirmed on my browser.
> 
> Also, I found GCP and AWS tracker endpoints:
> 
> * `gcp.api.snapchat.com/web-blizzard/web/send`:
>   
>   * Analytics/telemetry (Snapchat's "Blizzard" pipeline)
>   * Called 5× during login; protobuf POST; different subdomain from the already-blocked `web.snapchat.com/web-blizzard/web/send`
> * `aws.api.snapchat.com/snapchat.cdp.cof.CircumstancesService/targetingQuery`:
>   
>   * CDP audience targeting; gRPC-web; called 2×
> * `aws.api.snapchat.com/snapchat.ab.exposure_service.ExposureService/BatchUpdateAbExposure`:
>   
>   * A/B test exposure logging
>   * gRPC-web; payload includes experiment key `GOOGLE_OAUTH_CONSUMER_LOGIN_AB__188607`
> 
> Thanks for your reporting 😇 Please do not close this issue until it is applied to master.
> 
> Note
> 
> This issue is a continuation of [#215701](https://github.com/AdguardTeam/AdguardFilters/pull/215701)

thankyou @mu-hun

### Comment
> 🔹If i set dns service to dns.adguard-dns.com or family.adguard-dns.com or unfiltered server in device settings i can browse all apps except amazon (india) app normally. Even amazon website (.in/.com) loads properly in browser. Amazon app shows an unending loading screen as shown in attached screenshots.
> 🔹I also noticed some difficulty occasionally while downloading ad-ons in Firefox browser.
> 🔹 I tried device restart etc. but it does not fix this issue.
> 🔹 Even dns check shows server is connected (singapore).
> 🔹 Using alternate services like quad9 or controlD works fine. Even with private dns turned off, this issue does not happen.
> 🔹 This issue has been happening from past 4 days.

comment.body: Hi, you've mentioned that you have the problem even with Non-filtering AdGuard DNS. What country have you visited the website from? Could you also check it via AdGuard for Android? There you can leave only DNS module, then enable Non-filtering AdGuard DNS.

issue.body:
### Issue URL (Anti Adblock Script) [https://img.verb.tw/home/upload/](https://adguardteam.github.io/AnonymousRedirect/redirect.html?url=https%3A%2F%2Fimg.verb.tw%2Fhome%2Fupload%2F) ### Comment > Issue: The site uses a bait-element technique to detect ad blockers and blocks the upload feature when an ad blocker is active. > > How it works: > > The page runs a JavaScript snippet that: > > 1. Checks if adsbygoogle.js was blocked (by looking for script[src*="adsbygoogle"] in the DOM). > 2. Creates a hidden div off-screen with class names commonly targeted by ad blocker filter lists: adsbygoogle ad-slot ad-banner textads banner-ads, plus fake data-ad-client and data-ad-slot attributes. > 3. After 500ms, checks whether the bait element was removed from the DOM, or has offsetHeight/offsetWidth === 0, or has display:none / visibility:hidden applied by the ad blocker. > 4. If any condition is true, the upload UI is hidden and replaced with a message: "Ad blocker detected - ads are our only income, please disable your ad blocker and refresh." > > The bait element is never a real ad - it is purely a decoy used to detect and penalize users with ad blockers. ### Screenshots
Screenshot 1 ![Screenshot 1](https://cdn.adguardcdn.com/sitereports/jm7g57i37fcc80c8o08ocwck880o8ggqxp83s4g0g0.png?nc=1)
